title: Chunking, Segmentation, Document Structure, and Identity · Contract Workshop
summary: A retrieval engineer and domain partner incrementally build structure-aware chunking experiment, change one query, document, tenant, language, model, index, or policy condition, and keep earlier Python, provenance, accessibility, security, evaluation, and evidence invariants active. Planned artifact: Build a complete first version of structure-aware chunking experiment while every earlier competency remains active. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, ca…
objectives: Compare token, sentence, paragraph, section, semantic, table, code, and layout boundaries against the target retrieval unit.
Measure how chunk size and overlap affect boundary recall, redundancy, context cost, index size, and citation clarity.
Carry titles, heading paths, lists, tables, code language, captions, effective dates, and access metadata into chunk representation.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""rag-retrieval-augmented-generation cumulative evidence-grounded retrieval portfolio.

Browser execution uses deterministic pure-Python models and fixed fixtures only.
Native ML packages, files, models, vector stores, provider APIs, networks, credentials,
OCR, load, faults, recovery systems, and production effects require authorized transfer gates.
"""

from dataclasses import dataclass


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class RagEvidence:
    corpus_version: str
    document_id: str
    chunk_id: str
    query_id: str
    source_span: str


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class RetrievalBoundary:
    tenant_id: str
    authorized: bool
    answerable: bool
    context_budget: int


def portfolio_ready() -> str:
    return "Python RAG engineering portfolio ready"

steps.title: RAG and Python Commit to the mission model · Workshop Rag Chunk Unit Boundaries
steps.instruction:
workshop case — A workshop RAG engineering team handles case 3433 during a regulated document deletion request. Build deterministic Python evidence to choose structure-aware chunks, bounded overlap, stable identity, and parent-child evidence; keep browser execution pure, deterministic, bounded, and free of model, vector-database, network, file, credential, and host effects; then insert an untrusted instruction into a relevant document and prove it remains data. Browser code uses pure bounded models and fixed fixtures only. Native Python packages, embedding or generation models, vector databases, files, networks, credentials, provider APIs, OCR, load, faults, recovery systems, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Compare token, sentence, paragraph, section, semantic, table, code, and layout boundaries against the target retrieval unit.

content.code:
# Evidence: rag-stable-chunk-identity-67fwhu
# Competency: rag-stable-chunk-identity.
# Case 3698: a multimodal maintenance-manual release.
# Operating constraint: retain corpus, document, chunk, model, index, prompt, query, and evaluation version identity.
# Changed case: insert an untrusted instruction into a relevant document and prove it remains data.
def evidence_rag_stable_chunk_identity_67fwhu(token_count: int = 920, chunk_size: int = 240, overlap: int = 40, boundary_count: int = 5, parent_linked: bool = True) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_67fwhu = "67fwhu-uhwf76-3698"
    assert evidence_variant_67fwhu
    evidence_axes_67fwhu = (
        "corpus67fwhu",
        "queryuhwf76",
        "tenant3698",
        "index67fwhu3698",
        "promptuhwf763698",
        "evaluation369867fwhu",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_67fwhu)) == 6
    if token_count <= 0 or chunk_size <= 0:
        return False, 0, "chunk-input"
    if overlap < 0 or overlap >= chunk_size:
        return False, overlap, "overlap-budget"
    if boundary_count < 1 or not parent_linked:
        return False, boundary_count, "document-structure"
    stride = chunk_size - overlap
    chunks = 1 + max(0, (token_count - chunk_size + stride - 1) // stride)
    assert chunks >= 1
    return True, chunks, "structure-aware-chunks"
